Title: Micro Dashboard - BE for RLs
Description:
1. All - Everything that is not in Stocked or Closed on status
2. New - Lines that are in created status and sub status of “On Order” or blank.
3. Received - SLs in Received or Pre-Received sub status
4. QC Assigned - SLs that have QC Inspector assigned and in status of “Created”
5. In QC - SLs in QC Started status
6. Inspected - SLs in Inspected status and available qty > 0
7. Failed Inspection - SLs in inspected status and sub status of failed

Title: Inbound shipments changes
Description:
* Rename "Arrival Check" → "Add Arrival" everywhere it appears. - DONE
* "Add Arrival" entry points — trigger from PO, PO Line. No line pre-selection needed. Always operates at PO scope. - DONE

* Shipment Details accordion on Stock Line — add a accordion inside the Stock Line detail. Shows the shipment associated with that stock line.
* Shipments tab on Purchase order view : Add shipments tab on the purchase order view after the PO line and before the receiving lines
* Warehouse shipments view — Rename the receiving lines on left nav to receiving. Maintain same tab structure as PO (PO Lines, Shipments, Receiving Lines). Apply a default hidden filter to exclude unapproved POs. Warehouse only sees approved purchase orders - DONE

Title: Requirement Source Sync
Description:
Sales receive different GP levels based on the Requirement Source they enter. However, I have observed the following issue: 

Even when “Email – not related to WebRFQ” is selected and saved in APEX, the order is sent to xCRM, where the source is automatically changed to “MyChip1.”  - ✅ (https://chip1.xchangecrm.com/web/sales/so/update?ID=207266&SO_CODE_=0&COMPANY_CODE_=4)

If any updates are later made in xCRM and saved, the order is synced back to APEX, and the source is again overwritten to “MyChip1.” 

The problem is that when the Requirement Source is set to MyChip1 (or other sources like WebRFQ, Digipart, etc.), Sales receive little or no GP. 
They only receive the correct GP when the source is “Email – not related to WebRFQ.” 

As a result, Sales are becoming increasingly concerned, especially since we are not able to correct the Requirement Source after the order has been saved. 

 

https://crm.chip1.com/sales/orders/69dce85aa48d367506cff3c4

Title: Ready to Ship Statuses
Description:
Validate the automated status update flows, especially the ones to pay attention to are

1. When Stock Line is updated to Inspected and passed/failed, SO Line should reflect as Inspected → Passed/Failed✅
2. When Stock line is in “QC Started” then mark status “Procured” → QC Started✅
3. When Stock Line is updated to “Released for shipping” it should mark SO line as “Ready to Ship”. If more than one SL is allocated then add partial flag to SO Line status.
4. When Stock Line is updated to “On Hold” then SO Line status should be “Inspected” → On Hold
5. When Shipment is created, SO Line should be ‘Ready to Ship” → “Shipment Created”
6. If Shipment in “Ready to ship” then mark status as “Ready for Ship” → Shipment Pending
7. If Shipment in “Shipped” then mark status as “Shipped” → Shipped

Title: Attach exiting PO to SR
Description:
Implement a feature to attach an existing PO to an SR. This should primarily be treated associated the SR to PO and to SO like what we do for create flow but just that it is an existing PO. All the allocation flow needs to be run with this assocaition. 

It will address multiple use cases e.g., 

1. Allowing users to buy for multiple SRs 
2. Allow a PO created in xcrm to attach to SR
3. Allow old stock and PO to be associated with SR.



This should be in the FAB action of “Attach PO”. 



The current “Attach PO” should be changed to

1. “Add line to Purchase Order” (or Add line to PO)
2. In the Create new PO action, we can also add an option to Add line to PO similar to that of SR creation
